title: ServiceOrder v1
description: Machine-readable schema for one buyer-facing purchase intent referencing a standing service offer. In hard MVP this artifact is bridged by the host into the current procurement substrate rather than bypassing it. The buyer computes `request/units` explicitly; the host must not infer economic meaning by parsing human-readable pricing labels.

order/id.description: Stable identifier of this buyer-side service order.

created-at.description: Timestamp when the order became auditable.

buyer/node-id.description: Node acting as the buyer-side orchestrator for this order.

buyer/subject-kind.description: Kind of accountable buyer subject that authorizes the order.

buyer/subject-id.description: Identifier of the accountable buyer subject.

buyer/operator-participant-id.description: Optional participant subject operating on behalf of the buyer subject, for example the custodian of an organization purchase.

provider/node-id.description: Provider-side serving node expected to fulfill the order.

provider/participant-id.description: Provider-side accountable subject expected to stand behind the later procurement execution.

offer/id.description: Standing service offer selected by the buyer.

offer/seq.description: Standing-offer sequence observed by the buyer when the order was prepared. Hard MVP host bridge MUST reject stale mismatches against the active catalog.

service/type.description: Service category expected by the buyer. The host validates it against the referenced standing offer.

request/units.description: Requested number of billable units under the referenced standing offer. The buyer computes this quantity explicitly from its own domain input.

request/input.description: Structured buyer-side request input carried into the host-owned bridge.

request/output-constraints.description: Optional buyer-side output expectations narrower than or equal to the referenced standing offer.

pricing/max-amount.description: Maximum total price in minor units the buyer is willing to accept for this order. When `pricing/currency = ORC`, the value uses ORC minor units with fixed scale `2`.

pricing/currency.description: Settlement unit or currency symbol expected by the buyer.

delivery/requested-by.description: Optional stricter delivery expectation imposed by the buyer. The host must reject values that exceed offer bounds or policy.

workflow/run-id.description: Optional buyer-side workflow run identifier, for example from `Arca`.

workflow/phase-id.description: Optional buyer-side workflow phase identifier, for example one phase within a recurring orchestration.

lineage/upstream-refs.description: Optional refs to upstream artifacts or prior workflow outputs that justify this purchase.

policy_annotations.description: Optional buyer-local or federation-local annotations that do not redefine the core purchase semantics.

alg.description: Signature algorithm used by the buyer-side accountable subject.
